assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels — including that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable carries a full SLA, and clearly distinguishes minor version vs patch version differences between the channels
assertions.reason: The response mentions stability differences but doesn't explain SLA differences between channels or distinguish between minor vs patch version handling

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states 'No automatic upgrade to 1.32 stable' and discusses that changes don't happen immediately

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: The response mentions potential downgrade if 1.32 isn't available on Stable but doesn't warn about patch delays if 1.30 isn't available on Stable

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: The response provides the specific gcloud container clusters update command with --release-channel=stable flag

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: The response includes the gcloud container get-server-config command to check available versions on Stable channel

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: The response mentions 'Future updates will be less frequent but more thoroughly tested' and discusses slower update cadence

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: The response does not mention the Extended channel option or its cost implications

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response recommends planning during low-traffic periods and coordinating with the team
